---
title: "Accessing CDS BGLAD"
author: "Jason Taylor"
date: "2023-11-07"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Accessing CDS BGLAD.}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

<style>
table {
    max-width: 100%;
    max-height: 600px;
    overflow: scroll;
}
thead{
    position: sticky;
    top: 0px;
    background-color: #fff;
}
</style>


## Purpose

The purpose of this feature is to offer users sequences and sequence alignments precomputed from V-Quest and IgBLAST.

## Our sources

The sequences presented here have been aquired from the Los Alamos National Laboratory (LANL) and select publications.

## Usage caveats

STUB

## Understanding the `DataSpaceMabMetadata` object

DataSpace associates germline alignments with unique heavy and light chain sequences and each of is assigned a unique ID. Separately, metadata for antibodies is tracked in DataSpace in the `mabmetadata` table, where each antibody is also given a unique ID. Sequences and antibody metadata can then be associated with each other via a keyed relationship in the `antibody_sequence` table. In this way CDS treats antibody sequences as antibody metadata, which is why we have chosen to build this R API so that alignment data and sequences are loaded by request to a `DataSpaceMabMetadata` object. 

The `DataSpaceMabMetadata` object can be created multiple ways. 

* via a `DataSpaceConnection` object
* via a `DataSpaceMab` object

The easiest way to access antibody metadata, including sequences and metadata is through the `DataSpaceConnection` object.

### Accessing BGLAD via a `DataSpaceConnection` object

Like all DataSpaceR connections, one must have an account first and have properly set up their netrc file.

```r
library(DataSpaceR)

con <- connectDS()
met <- con$getMabMetadata()
```


## Tutorial for using sequence and alignment data

Connect to DataSpace via the R API.


```r
library(DataSpaceR)
con <- connectDS()
```

Access metadata for a subset of neutralizing antibody assay (NAb) data...


```r

con$filterMabGrid("mab_mixture", "VRC01")
mab <- con$getMab()
mab$mabMetadata$loadAlignments()
met <- mab$mabMetadata
met
#> <DataSpaceMabMetadata>
#>   URL: https://dataspace-staging.cavd.org
#>   User: jmtaylor@fredhutch.org
#>   Summary:
#>     - 1 mabs
#>   MAbs in metadata: 
#>      VRC01
#>   MAb sequences available:
#>      VRC01
#>   MAb sequences loaded:
#>      VRC01
```

...or from all available metadata.


```r

met <- con$getMabMetadata()
met$loadAlignments()
met 
#> <DataSpaceMabMetadata>
#>   URL: https://dataspace-staging.cavd.org
#>   User: jmtaylor@fredhutch.org
#>   Summary:
#>     - 932 mabs
#>   MAbs in metadata: 
#>      PGT128, 4E10, AB-000440-1, AB-000441-1, AB-000442-1, AB-000443-1, AB-0...
#>   MAb sequences available:
#>      PGT128, b12, PGDM1400, PG16, CH27, CH28, CH31, CH58, CH59, PG9, PGT121...
#>   MAb sequences loaded:
#>      PGT128, b12, PGDM1400, PG16, CH27, CH28, CH31, CH58, CH59, PG9, PGT121...
```

As shown above, the metadata object does not contain sequences and alignments by default but must be loaded to the object manually.

The `loadAlignements` method can also take a charactor vector argument of which mAbs to load sequences and alignments for. In the example below we load sequence and alignment data for all the mAbs where `mab_donor_clade` is listed as `A`.


```r

met <- con$getMabMetadata()
mabIds <- met$mabMetadata[mab_donor_clade == "A" & sequence_available == TRUE, mab_id]
met$loadAlignments(mabIds)
met 
#> <DataSpaceMabMetadata>
#>   URL: https://dataspace-staging.cavd.org
#>   User: jmtaylor@fredhutch.org
#>   Summary:
#>     - 932 mabs
#>   MAbs in metadata: 
#>      PGT128, 4E10, AB-000440-1, AB-000441-1, AB-000442-1, AB-000443-1, AB-0...
#>   MAb sequences available:
#>      PGT128, b12, PGDM1400, PG16, CH27, CH28, CH31, CH58, CH59, PG9, PGT121...
#>   MAb sequences loaded:
#>      CH31, PGT121, PGT123, CH01, PGC14, VRC-CH30, VRC-CH32, VRC-CH33, VRC-C...
```

Once sequences and alignments are loaded, there are several active bindings available to access those data:


```r

met$sequences
met$alignments
met$topCalls
met$alleleSequences
met$runInformation

```
 
Descriptions for the various fields in each of these objects can be found using the `variableDefinitions` active binding.


```r

met$variableDefinitions

```

A FASTA file can be exported from a DataSpaceMabMetadata object after alignments are loaded with the `loadAlignments()` method. By default, sequence headers are generated from DataSpace mAb metadata however, the original headers from the sequence source can be used instead by toggling the `orginalHeaders` argument.


```r

fasta <- mab$getFastaFromSequences()
writeLines(fasta, "<export_location.fasta>")

## or

fasta <- mab$getFastaFromSequences(orginalHeaders = TRUE)
writeLines(fasta, "<export_location.fasta>")

```
